title: Debug RBAC 403 Forbidden Error
description:
Diagnose and fix a 403 Forbidden error caused by a ServiceAccount missing permission to list Secrets.

`secret-reader-sa` has a RoleBinding granting only ConfigMap access. It tries to list Secrets and gets Forbidden. Add a `secret-lister` Role (get/list on secrets) and bind it to the SA.

**Common Pitfalls**: Secrets are in `apiGroups: [""]` (core API). Add a second RoleBinding — no need to modify the existing one. Kubernetes merges permissions from all bindings. Verify with `kubectl auth can-i` before and after.
steps: Apply the initial broken state (ServiceAccount, a Role for configmaps, and a RoleBinding) from the editor
Verify the SA cannot list secrets: kubectl auth can-i list secrets --as system:serviceaccount:default:secret-reader-sa (expect 'no')
Create a new Role named secret-lister with rules: apiGroups [""], resources ["secrets"], verbs ["list", "get"]
Create a new RoleBinding named secret-lister-binding that binds secret-lister to secret-reader-sa
Verify the SA can now list secrets: kubectl auth can-i list secrets --as system:serviceaccount:default:secret-reader-sa (expect 'yes')
Verify the SA still cannot delete secrets: kubectl auth can-i delete secrets --as system:serviceaccount:default:secret-reader-sa (expect 'no')
hints: kubectl auth can-i --list --as system:serviceaccount:default:secret-reader-sa shows all current permissions
You can have multiple RoleBindings for the same ServiceAccount — Kubernetes merges all granted permissions
Secrets are in the core API group: apiGroups: [""]
kubectl describe rolebinding <name> shows which subjects and roles are connected

editor_config.initial_code:
# --- INITIAL BROKEN STATE (already deployed) ---
# ServiceAccount with incomplete permissions
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: secret-reader-sa
  namespace: default
---
# Existing Role: only allows configmap access (NOT secrets)
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: configmap-only-reader
  namespace: default
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list"]
---
# Existing RoleBinding
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: configmap-binding
  namespace: default
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: secret-reader-sa
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: configmap-only-reader
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
# --- YOUR FIX: Add the missing permissions below ---
# Create a new Role named secret-lister
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: secret-lister
  namespace: default
rules:
- apiGroups: # TODO: [""] for core API
  resources: # TODO: ["secrets"]
  verbs: # TODO: ["list", "get"] — no delete!
---
# Create a new RoleBinding
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: secret-lister-binding
  namespace: default
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: secret-reader-sa
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: secret-lister
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: secret-reader-sa
  namespace: default
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: configmap-only-reader
  namespace: default
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: configmap-binding
  namespace: default
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: secret-reader-sa
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: configmap-only-reader
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: secret-lister
  namespace: default
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets"]
  verbs: ["list", "get"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: secret-lister-binding
  namespace: default
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: secret-reader-sa
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: secret-lister
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml
echo "✓ Resources applied"

# ServiceAccount
kubectl get serviceaccount secret-reader-sa -n default > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ ServiceAccount secret-reader-sa exists" || { echo "✗ secret-reader-sa not found"; exit 1; }

# secret-lister Role
kubectl get role secret-lister -n default > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ Role secret-lister exists" || { echo "✗ Role secret-lister not found"; exit 1; }

# Verify secret-lister rules
resources=$(kubectl get role secret-lister -n default -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].resources[*]}')
if echo "$resources" | grep -q "secrets"; then
  echo "✓ Role secret-lister targets secrets"
else
  echo "✗ Role secret-lister should target secrets. Found: $resources"
  exit 1
fi

verbs=$(kubectl get role secret-lister -n default -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].verbs[*]}')
if echo "$verbs" | grep -q "list"; then
  echo "✓ Role secret-lister includes list verb"
else
  echo "✗ Role secret-lister must include list verb. Found: $verbs"
  exit 1
fi

if echo "$verbs" | grep -q "delete"; then
  echo "✗ Role secret-lister should NOT include delete verb"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Role secret-lister does NOT include delete verb"

# secret-lister-binding RoleBinding
kubectl get rolebinding secret-lister-binding -n default > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ RoleBinding secret-lister-binding exists" || { echo "✗ RoleBinding secret-lister-binding not found"; exit 1; }

# Test can list secrets NOW
can_list=$(kubectl auth can-i list secrets --as system:serviceaccount:default:secret-reader-sa -n default)
if [ "$can_list" = "yes" ]; then
  echo "✓ secret-reader-sa CAN now list secrets"
else
  echo "✗ secret-reader-sa still cannot list secrets (got: $can_list)"
  exit 1
fi

# Test still cannot delete secrets
can_delete=$(kubectl auth can-i delete secrets --as system:serviceaccount:default:secret-reader-sa -n default)
if [ "$can_delete" = "no" ]; then
  echo "✓ secret-reader-sa CANNOT delete secrets (correct)"
else
  echo "✗ secret-reader-sa should NOT be able to delete secrets (got: $can_delete)"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify original configmap permission still works
can_cm=$(kubectl auth can-i list configmaps --as system:serviceaccount:default:secret-reader-sa -n default)
if [ "$can_cm" = "yes" ]; then
  echo "✓ secret-reader-sa still CAN list configmaps (existing permission preserved)"
else
  echo "✗ configmap permission was lost (got: $can_cm)"
  exit 1
fi

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
